title: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 1
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1-13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.

Primary Education in the Age of AI

A

In many primary schools, artificial intelligence is no longer treated as a distant technology belonging only to older students or office workers. Simple tutoring programs, speech-to-text tools and image generators have begun to appear in classrooms for children who are still learning basic reading and number skills. The most useful debate is not whether AI should be allowed into primary education at all, but what it should be allowed to do. Used carelessly, it may encourage shortcuts. Used carefully, it can become a limited assistant that helps teachers notice needs they might otherwise miss.

B

For younger pupils, the first principle is that foundational literacy, numeracy and handwriting should remain central. Children still need to decode words, form letters, count physical objects and explain ideas in their own voices. An AI system can provide extra practice at different levels, but it cannot replace the slow confidence built when a child struggles, tries again and receives encouragement from an adult. Primary education is partly about knowledge, but it is also about attention, patience and trust. These are not features that can be downloaded into software.

C

Teachers may gain the clearest benefit from AI outside the most visible moments of classroom teaching. A program can help sort quiz results, suggest reading materials, draft feedback or translate a message for parents. These tasks do not remove the teacher's judgement; they make space for it. If a teacher spends less time rewriting routine comments, more time can be given to discussing misconceptions with pupils. However, the teacher must remain responsible for checking any output, because AI can produce errors that look fluent and authoritative.

D

There are strong reasons to be cautious. Young children learn through talk, movement and play, not only through answers on a screen. If every mistake is instantly corrected by a program, pupils may lose opportunities to explain their reasoning to classmates. There is also a risk that children will begin to see quick production as the same thing as understanding. For this reason, some schools ask pupils to use AI only after they have attempted a task independently. The aim is to protect effort, not to pretend that new tools do not exist.

E

Equity is another concern. Wealthier schools may be able to buy safer platforms, train staff and provide reliable devices, while less well-funded schools may depend on free tools with weaker privacy controls. AI systems can also carry cultural or language assumptions that do not fit every classroom. A child who speaks a minority language at home may receive feedback that is technically correct but educationally unhelpful. Before primary schools use such systems widely, they need clear rules about data, bias and the kinds of work that should never be handed over to a machine.

F

Assessment will probably have to change. If homework can be partly generated by AI, teachers will need more evidence of how pupils think. This may mean more oral explanations, short tasks completed in class, and questions that ask children to describe the process they followed. None of this requires schools to reject AI completely. It suggests a more demanding goal: children should learn that a tool can help them, but it cannot do their learning for them. In the age of AI, primary education may become more human, if schools are clear about what only humans can teach.

Questions 1-7

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?

In boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet, write

YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

1 AI tools are currently useful only for older students and office workers.
2 The main question is how AI should be used in primary schools, not whether it should ever be used.
3 Primary pupils should continue to develop basic literacy and numeracy skills.
4 One primary-school AI trial doubled pupils' mathematics scores within six months.
5 The passage claims that AI can completely replace the encouragement children receive from adults.
6 AI may reduce the amount of routine written work teachers have to do.
7 Assessment should include evidence of children's thinking process.

Questions 8-13

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 8-13 on your answer sheet.

AI and young learners

At the centre of learning, and careful approach 8....................

May give, and pupils 9....................

Answer signal 10....................

Screen-based answers are not enough, and answer signal 11....................

Schools need policies on privacy and .................... prior to classroom rollout of AI tools 12....................

Assessment may ask, and pupils 13....................
rawAnswerKey:
Question 1: AI tools are currently useful only for older students and office workers.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: artificial intelligence is no longer treated as a distant technology belonging only to older students or office workers. Simple tutoring programs, speech-to-text tools and image generators have begun to appear in classrooms

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะโจทย์บอกว่า AI useful only for older students and office workers แต่ passage ปฏิเสธความคิดนี้ด้วยคำว่า no longer treated as a distant technology belonging only to older students or office workers และบอกว่าเครื่องมือ AI เริ่มปรากฏใน primary classrooms แล้ว ดังนั้นคำว่า only ในโจทย์ขัดกับข้อมูลใน passage โดยตรง

Paraphrased Vocabulary: currently useful only = belonging only; older students and office workers = older students or office workers; begun to appear in classrooms = used with younger pupils too; answer signal = NO

Question 2:The main question is how AI should be used in primary schools, not whether it should ever be used.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: The most useful debate is not whether AI should be allowed into primary education at all, but what it should be allowed to do.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะโจทย์บอกว่า issue หลักไม่ใช่ว่า AI ควรถูกใช้หรือไม่ แต่คือควรถูกใช้อย่างไร ซึ่งตรงกับ passage ที่บอกว่า debate ที่มีประโยชน์ที่สุดคือ not whether AI should be allowed แต่ what it should be allowed to do คำว่า how AI should be used ในโจทย์จึงเทียบกับ what it should be allowed to do ใน passage ได้ชัดเจน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: main question = most useful debate; how AI should be used = what it should be allowed to do; not whether it should ever be used = not whether AI should be allowed; answer signal = YES

Question 3: Primary pupils should continue to develop basic literacy and numeracy skills.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: the first principle is that foundational literacy, numeracy and handwriting should remain central.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะโจทย์บอกว่าเด็กประถมควรยังพัฒนา basic literacy and numeracy skills ต่อไป ซึ่งตรงกับ passage ที่บอกว่า foundational literacy, numeracy and handwriting should remain central คำว่า should continue to develop ในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับ should remain central ใน passage จึงเป็น YES

Paraphrased Vocabulary: basic = foundational; literacy and numeracy skills = literacy, numeracy and handwriting; should continue = should remain central; answer signal = YES

Question 4: One primary-school AI trial doubled pupils' mathematics scores within six months.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: An AI system can provide extra practice at different levels

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage กล่าวเพียงว่า AI can provide extra practice at different levels และกล่าวถึง numeracy แบบทั่วไป แต่ไม่ได้พูดถึง trial ใด ๆ ไม่ได้บอกว่า scores doubled และไม่ได้ระบุช่วงเวลา six months ข้อมูลเหล่านี้เป็นรายละเอียดเฉพาะที่ไม่มีใน passage จึงไม่สามารถตอบ YES หรือ NO ได้

Paraphrased Vocabulary: primary-school AI trial = not stated; doubled scores = not stated; mathematics = numeracy context only; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 5: The passage claims that AI can completely replace the encouragement children receive from adults.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: it cannot replace the slow confidence built when a child struggles, tries again and receives encouragement from an adult.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะโจทย์กล่าวว่า AI can completely replace encouragement from adults แต่ passage ระบุชัดว่า it cannot replace ความมั่นใจที่เกิดจากเด็กพยายามใหม่และได้รับ encouragement from an adult คำว่า completely replace ในโจทย์ขัดกับ cannot replace ใน passage โดยตรง จึงตอบ NO

Paraphrased Vocabulary: completely replace = replace; encouragement children receive from adults = receives encouragement from an adult; cannot = contradicts can; answer signal = NO

Question 6: AI may reduce the amount of routine written work teachers have to do.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: A program can help sort quiz results, suggest reading materials, draft feedback or translate a message for parents. These tasks do not remove the teacher's judgement; they make space for it.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะโจทย์บอกว่า AI อาจลดงานเขียน routine ของครู ซึ่ง passage ยกตัวอย่างว่าโปรแกรมช่วย draft feedback หรือ translate a message for parents ได้ และอธิบายว่า tasks เหล่านี้ make space for teacher judgement หมายถึงช่วยลดภาระงานบางส่วนเพื่อให้ครูมีเวลามากขึ้น ดังนั้นความหมายของโจทย์ตรงกับ passage

Paraphrased Vocabulary: reduce routine written work = draft feedback / translate a message; teachers have to do = teacher tasks; may = can help; answer signal = YES

Question 7:Assessment should include evidence of children's thinking process.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: teachers will need more evidence of how pupils think. This may mean more oral explanations, short tasks completed in class, and questions that ask children to describe the process they followed.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะโจทย์บอกว่า assessment ควรรวม evidence ของ thinking process ของเด็ก ซึ่งตรงกับ passage ที่บอกว่า teachers will need more evidence of how pupils think และอาจใช้ questions ที่ให้เด็ก describe the process they followed คำว่า thinking process ในโจทย์จึงมาจาก how pupils think และ process they followed ใน passage

Paraphrased Vocabulary: assessment should include = teachers will need; evidence of thinking process = evidence of how pupils think / process they followed; children = pupils; answer signal = YES

Question 8: At the centre of learning, and careful approach ....................
Correct Answer: literacy

Accepted Answers: literacy

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: the first principle is that foundational literacy, numeracy and handwriting should remain central.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ literacy เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary ใช้โครงสร้างเหมือน passage ว่า foundational ____, numeracy and handwriting at the centre of learning ส่วน passage เขียนว่า foundational literacy, numeracy and handwriting should remain central คำว่า at the centre เทียบกับ remain central ได้ และคำที่ต้องเติมคือ literacy เป็นคำเดียวจาก passage


Paraphrased Vocabulary: of learning and careful approach = the first principle is that foundational literacy numeracy and handwriting; answer signal = literacy

Question 9: May give, and pupils ....................
Correct Answer: levels

Accepted Answers: levels

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: An AI system can provide extra practice at different levels

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ levels เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary ถามว่า AI may give pupils practice at different ____ และ passage ใช้คำตรง ๆ ว่า provide extra practice at different levels คำว่า may give เทียบกับ can provide ได้ และคำตอบต้องเป็นคำเดียวจาก passage คือ levels


Paraphrased Vocabulary: may give and pupils = an ai system can provide extra practice at different levels; answer signal = levels

Question 10: Answer signal ....................
Correct Answer: feedback

Accepted Answers: feedback

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: A program can help sort quiz results, suggest reading materials, draft feedback or translate a message for parents.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ feedback เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary บอกว่า AI can help teachers draft ____ และ passage ระบุว่า A program can help ... draft feedback คำว่า for parents or pupils ใน summary สรุปจากบริบท feedback และ message for parents แต่คำที่เติมตรงจาก passage คือ feedback ซึ่งเป็นคำเดียวและเหมาะกับกริยา draft


Paraphrased Vocabulary: answer signal = a program can help sort quiz results suggest reading materials; answer signal = feedback

Question 11: Screen-based answers are not enough, and answer signal ....................
Correct Answer: movement

Accepted Answers: movement

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: Young children learn through talk, movement and play, not only through answers on a screen.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ movement เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary ระบุว่า children learn through talk, ____ and play และ passage ใช้ประโยคตรงกันว่า Young children learn through talk, movement and play คำว่า screen-based answers are not enough ใน summary สรุปจาก not only through answers on a screen ดังนั้นคำที่เติมคือ movement


Paraphrased Vocabulary: not enough and answer signal = young children learn through talk movement and play not only; answer signal = movement

Question 12: Schools need policies on privacy and .................... prior to classroom rollout of AI tools.
Correct Answer: bias

Accepted Answers: bias

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: Before primary schools use such systems widely, they need clear rules about data, bias and the kinds of work that should never be handed over to a machine.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ bias เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary บอกว่า schools need rules about privacy and ____ before using AI widely ส่วน passage ระบุว่า before primary schools use such systems widely, they need clear rules about data, bias and kinds of work คำว่า privacy ใน summary paraphrase มาจาก data/privacy controls ในย่อหน้าเดียวกัน และคำที่เติมคือ bias


Paraphrased Vocabulary: policies = clear rules; privacy = data; classroom rollout = use such systems widely; answer = bias

Question 13: Assessment may ask, and pupils ....................
Correct Answer: process

Accepted Answers: process

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - Primary Education in the Age of AI

Exact Portion: questions that ask children to describe the process they followed.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ process เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary ถามว่า assessment may ask pupils to explain the ____ they followed และ passage ระบุว่า questions that ask children to describe the process they followed คำว่า explain เทียบกับ describe ได้ และ pupils เทียบกับ children ได้ คำตอบจึงเป็น process ซึ่งเป็นคำเดียวจาก passage


Paraphrased Vocabulary: assessment may ask and pupils = questions that ask children to describe the process they followed; answer signal = process
